---
name: rule-initiative
description: Use for initiative values, encounter deck timing, ties, surprise, and initiative-changing effects.
---

# Rule: Initiative

## Scope

Use this rule for initiative values, encounter deck windows, tie behavior, and when initiative changes take effect.

## Consolidated Rule

Initiative determines which encounter deck range activates a creature. Cards are range-based, not single-value sequencing. When a range card flips, all creatures in that range can act according to action economy rules.

## Setup and Activation

1. Determine encounter level band.
2. Build 10-card encounter deck for that band.
3. Shuffle and flip cards sequentially.
4. On each range card, activate all matching creatures.

If multiple creatures share initiative:
- enemy groups can activate one-by-one on that card,
- players with identical initiative can resolve in clockwise order or any agreed player order.

## Surprise and Skips

Surprised creatures skip their first initiative card in the first combat round only.

## Initiative Modification

- Equipment and training can alter effective initiative.
- Weapon profile changes affect initiative at start of next round.
- Initiative bonuses from training apply per trained weapon category when using that category.

## Adjudication Notes

- Initiative windows are card-driven; do not convert to strict descending initiative count.
- If a creature’s initiative changes mid-round, apply at next round unless a rule explicitly says immediate.
- Keep initiative cards visible to avoid timing disputes.
